questions.text: What was the name of the secret kitten found by Ravi and Meena?
questions.explanation: Ravi named the kitten Mahendra-varma Pallava Poonai (Mahendran for short).

questions.text: How did Ravi get milk for the kitten?
options: He stole it from the neighbor
questions.explanation: Ravi told Paati he was hungry and drank most of the milk to prove it, saving some for the cat.

questions.text: Lalli was learning to play which musical instrument?
questions.explanation: Lalli was taking lessons from a music master to play the violin.

questions.text: Who was sitting on the doorstep when the children looked out?
questions.explanation: A beggar had been coming to the house for a week and was resting under the neem tree.

questions.text: Why did the children want to give the beggar a pair of chappals?
options: His feet were blistered from the hot road
They didn't like his old ones
It was a gift for his birthday
questions.explanation: The summer heat had made the road very hot, and the beggar had no footwear, causing blisters.

questions.text: Whose chappals did the children finally give to the beggar?
questions.explanation: The children found a pair of old-looking, sturdy chappals in the verandah which belonged to the music master.

questions.text: What did the music master call the children after his chappals went missing?
questions.explanation: He was very angry and compared them to 'monkeys', specifically Hanuman's incarnations.

questions.text: To compensate the music master, Rukku Mani gave him:
questions.explanation: She gave him Gopu Mama's brand new chappals that he hardly ever wore.

questions.text: Rukku Mani laughed at the end because:
options: She imagined Gopu Mama's reaction when he returned
questions.explanation: She wondered what she would tell Gopu Mama when he asked for his chappals after coming home.

questions.text: The theme of the story 'A Gift of Chappals' is:
questions.explanation: The story highlights the innocent and empathetic nature of children who try to help a beggar.
